picks.rationale: The Cleveland Cavaliers are a home favorite in Game 4, facing a 1-2 series deficit, a situation that historically sees home favorites cover at a 55% ATS rate. The Detroit Pistons are dealing with key injuries to Kevin Huerter (questionable - adductor strain) and Caris LeVert (questionable - heel contusion), which impacts their depth and offensive spacing. Cleveland has no reported injuries and will be highly motivated to even the series at home. The Cavaliers have also shown to be the superior shooting team in the series and are expected to continue converting at a higher clip as they find more effective lineup combinations.

picks.game: Oklahoma City Thunder @ Los Angeles Lakers
picks.rationale: The Oklahoma City Thunder are undefeated in the playoffs (7-0) and hold a commanding 3-0 series lead, winning all three games by at least 18 points, showcasing consistent dominance. The Los Angeles Lakers are severely hampered by the absence of their star player Luka Doncic, who is out with a hamstring injury, significantly impacting their offensive production. Oklahoma City's superior depth, athleticism, and relentless second-half execution have consistently overwhelmed the Lakers, even when Los Angeles has managed to compete early in games. The line movement from -10.5 to -11.5, coupled with heavy public money on the Thunder, further indicates strong market confidence in their ability to cover this spread.

Two of the last three playoff games between the Pistons and Cavaliers have gone Under the total, suggesting a trend towards lower-scoring affairs in this series. The Detroit Pistons are facing potential absences from key offensive contributors Kevin Huerter and Caris LeVert due to injury, which could limit their scoring efficiency. Detroit's defense has been effective throughout the series, notably holding Cleveland to 109 points in Game 3. With both teams likely to emphasize defensive intensity in a crucial Game 4, a tight, grind-it-out contest with fewer points is anticipated.

picks.game: Oklahoma City Thunder @ Los Angeles Lakers
picks.rationale: The Oklahoma City Thunder possess a potent offense, averaging 119.3 points per game over their last 10 outings and scoring 131 points in their dominant Game 3 victory against the Lakers. Despite the absence of Luka Doncic, the Lakers still managed to put up 108 points in Game 3, indicating some offensive capability. The total line has seen movement towards the Over, with public money favoring a higher-scoring game, suggesting market expectation for offensive output. The Thunder's ability to generate explosive second-half runs and their overall offensive efficiency, ranking fifth in the league in scoring, strongly supports the Over.

The Detroit Pistons currently hold a 2-1 series lead over the Cleveland Cavaliers, demonstrating their ability to win outright in this playoff matchup. Detroit has been in strong recent form, winning 6 of their last 10 games, including 4 of their last 5. While Cleveland is a home favorite, the Pistons have already secured road victories in this series, proving they can overcome the home-court advantage. The +140 moneyline offers significant value for a team that has consistently outperformed expectations and features a primary creator in Cade Cunningham, who is averaging 30.2 points and 7.7 assists per game in the playoffs.

The Colorado Avalanche, despite their 5-1 loss in Game 3, are a formidable road team with a 29-7-5 record this season. They boast an impressive 9-1-0 record in their last 10 games overall, indicating strong recent form. The Wild are significantly hampered by injuries to key players Joel Eriksson Ek and Jonas Brodin, both of whom are out for Game 4, which weakens their defensive structure. Colorado will be motivated for a strong bounce-back performance, as they have not had to rebound from a playoff loss until now. Public money has heavily backed the Avalanche, with 79% of moneyline bets on Colorado.

The series between the Avalanche and Wild has seen high-scoring affairs, with combined goals of 9, 7, and 6 in the first three games. Colorado leads the league in Goals Per Game (3.63), while Minnesota's power play is highly effective, ranking 3rd in the NHL at 25.2%. The total line has already climbed from 6 to 6.5, with 75% of public money favoring the Over, signaling sharp action towards a high-scoring contest. Given the offensive firepower of both teams and Minnesota's recent success on the power play, this game is poised to exceed the 6.5 goal total.

The Minnesota Wild have a strong historical trend as home underdogs against the Avalanche, winning 8 of their last 9 games in this situation and covering the puck line in 15 of their last 16. They are also 3-1 in their last four home meetings against Colorado. Coming off a dominant 5-1 victory in Game 3, the Wild demonstrated their ability to play with defensive intensity at home, allowing only 2.2 goals per game in the playoffs at Grand Casino Arena. Despite key injuries, their home advantage and recent performance suggest they can keep this game within one goal.

Kirill Kaprizov is a primary offensive threat for the Minnesota Wild, leading the team with 45 goals and 89 points in the regular season. He demonstrated his scoring prowess in Game 3, netting a goal and adding two power-play assists in the Wild's 5-1 victory. Kaprizov is confirmed to be active and playing for the Wild today. With Minnesota's potent power play (3rd in NHL) and Kaprizov's consistent production, he is a strong candidate to score a goal in Game 4.

Nathan MacKinnon is the offensive engine for the Colorado Avalanche, leading the team with 53 goals and 127 points in the regular season. He scored a power-play goal in Game 3, continuing his strong playoff performance with five goals in the postseason. MacKinnon is confirmed to be active and playing for the Avalanche today. Given Colorado's motivation to bounce back from their Game 3 loss and MacKinnon's consistent scoring ability, he is highly likely to find the back of the net.
